headline: Bakun Dam Resettlement Still Unpaid After 25 Years
context: Some 10,000 indigenous people displaced by the Bakun Dam in 1998-2000 have yet to receive full compensation promised by the federal government.

cards.big: 10,000 Bakun Dam evacuees still await full compensation since 1998
cards.sub: Government promised RM500 million but disbursed only RM180 million

cards.big: Sungai Asap resettlement has 6,500 residents in deteriorating longhouses
cards.sub: Housing built in 1998 has not been maintained and 40% show structural damage

cards.big: Each family promised 3 hectares of farmland but received only 1.2 hectares
cards.sub: Much allocated land proved unsuitable for the promised oil palm cultivation

cards.big: Bakun Dam generates 2,400MW but Sarawak receives only RM50 million annually
cards.sub: Sarawak Energy sells power to Peninsula via submarine cable at federal-set rates

cards.big: Bakun reveals whether displaced communities share in the wealth their sacrifice created
25 years without resettlement compensation. Bakun's displaced communities subsidised peninsula electricity.
